design_patterns: Soft delete support via deletedAt field

patterns_discovered.description: All tenant-scoped models include organizationId field with foreign key to Organization
Majority of models include deletedAt DateTime field for logical deletion
Models supporting user-facing URLs (Menu, Category, Product) include slug field with unique constraints per scope
Hierarchical and list-based models include sortOrder Int field for custom ordering
All price fields use Decimal(10, 2) type for financial accuracy
Menu.theme, Category.parent, and Product.nutritionInfo are optional relationships allowing flexible data modeling
System-managed timestamps and sensible defaults (isActive, isPublished, currency) reduce nullable fields

gotchas_discovered.description: Allergen model lacks organizationId, indicating it's platform-scoped rather than tenant-scoped. This differs from other models and may require special handling in multi-tenant queries.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document this as intentional design for cross-tenant allergen standardization

gotchas_discovered.description: Product.basePrice exists alongside ProductVariant.price, which could lead to pricing inconsistency if variant price is not set
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Establish clear pricing logic: determine if variant price overrides basePrice or if basePrice is mandatory fallback

gotchas_discovered.description: ProductVariant lacks a unique constraint on (productId, name), allowing duplicate variant names within same product
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Add @@unique([productId, name]) to prevent accidental duplication

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Cascade delete on variants and modifiers
gotchas_discovered.description: ProductVariant and ProductModifier use onDelete: Cascade, meaning deleting a Product deletes all variants/modifiers without soft-delete preservation
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Consider soft-delete alternative if historical variant/modifier data is needed for analytics

gotchas_discovered.description: Media.storage and MediaType/MediaStorage enums are referenced but enum definitions not provided in schema
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Define MediaType and MediaStorage enums in schema or separate file

gotchas_discovered.description: Menu model includes relation to QRCode[], but QRCode model is not defined in this schema file
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify QRCode model exists in another schema file or is defined elsewhere in Prisma configuration

gotchas_discovered.description: Product and ProductVariant include relations to OrderItem[], but OrderItem model is not defined in this schema file
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify OrderItem model exists in a separate schema file (likely order.prisma or similar)

gotchas_discovered.description: Product.gallery is String[] with no documented maximum size, potentially allowing unbounded growth
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document expected maximum gallery size or implement application-level validation

requirements_met: Menu entity with organization scope and publishing controls
Category entity with hierarchical self-referential support
Product entity with comprehensive menu item details
architectural_decisions: Cascade delete for variants and modifiers
SEO-friendly slug fields with scope-based uniqueness
JSON field for flexible settings storage
approach_outcome.implementation_completeness: Comprehensive with well-documented models and relationships

recommendations.recommendation: Verify external model references (QRCode, OrderItem) exist in related schema files
Define MediaType and MediaStorage enums referenced in Media model
recommendations.impact: Enables proper type safety for media storage operations

recommendations.recommendation: Add unique constraint on ProductVariant: @@unique([productId, name])
Document and enforce pricing logic for products with variants (basePrice vs variant price)
recommendations.impact: Prevents pricing inconsistencies in menu calculations

recommendations.recommendation: Consider implementing soft-delete for ProductVariant and ProductModifier instead of cascade delete
recommendations.impact: Enables historical tracking for analytics and audit trails

recommendations.recommendation: Document that Allergen is platform-scoped and not organization-scoped
recommendations.impact: Clarifies multi-tenancy model to future developers

recommendations.recommendation: Document maximum size for Product.gallery array and implement validation
Consider adding index on Product.organizationId for faster tenant isolation queries
recommendations.impact: Improves query performance in multi-tenant scenarios
